name: Chandra
description: Chandra (Sanskrit: चन्द्र, romanized: Chandra, lit. 'shining' or 'moon'), also known as Soma (Sanskrit: सोम), is the Hindu god of the Moon, and is associated with the night, plants and vegetation. He is one of the Navagraha (nine planets of Hinduism) and Dikpala (guardians of the directions).[5]

infobox.Other names: Soma, Chandrama, Shashank, Nishakara, Shashi, Mayank, Vidhu
infobox.Consort: Rohini (chief consort), and other 26 Nakshatra goddesses
infobox.Children: Budha, Varchas, Bhadra, Jyotsnakali[4] and other children

aliases: is the Hindu god of the Moon
